title: Voice Cloning and TTS for Preservation and Enrichment of Cultural Heritage
author: Culafic, Igor
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: ABSTRACT - This research presents a comparative analysis of modern voice cloning systems, focusing on their ability to generate high-quality speech from limited training data. The paper aims to demonstrate that carefully curated smaller datasets can produce superior results to larger, less structured datasets. The investigation of multiple state-of-the-art models, including Realtime Voice Cloning (RVC), Tortoise AI, Bark, and Coqui AI, establishes optimal data preparation protocols and identifies critical factors in training data quality, with particular emphasis on applications for the Montenegrin language and cultural preservation.
steps_raw: ABSTRACT - This research presents a comparative analysis of modern voice cloning systems, focusing on their ability to generate high-quality speech from limited training data. The paper aims to demonstrate that carefully curated smaller datasets can produce superior results to larger, less structured datasets. The investigation of multiple state-of-the-art models, including Realtime Voice Cloning (RVC), Tortoise AI, Bark, and Coqui AI, establishes optimal data preparation protocols and identifies critical factors in training data quality, with particular emphasis on applications for the Montenegrin language and cultural preservation.
